---
title: "Afternoon Track"
---

## Goal

Show advanced users how to build, inspect, deploy, and connect their own MCP server.

:::: {.lesson-grid}
::: {.lesson-panel}
### 1. [A smooth environment keeps the class moving](dev-environment.qmd)

Standardize the editor, Python tooling, and command rhythm.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 2. [MCP concepts should feel concrete](mcp-foundations.qmd)

Explain only the architecture needed to make the build legible.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 3. [Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern](build-server.qmd)

Turn plain Python functions into MCP tools with clear structure.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 4. [Deployment should feel like the next step](deploy-and-connect.qmd)

Walk from local server to remote endpoint to Copilot Studio connection.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 5. [Brainstorming works best after a concrete demo](brainstorming.qmd)

End with realistic team use cases rather than abstract ideas.
:::
::::

## Emphasis

The walkthrough should make the code transparent, not magical. The afternoon slide deck will therefore include line-by-line commentary, progressive code highlighting, and architecture diagrams.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Canonical stack

The baseline implementation uses the official MCP Python SDK with the `FastMCP` authoring pattern and a deployment shape suited for HTTP-based hosting.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Teaching rhythm

Keep the afternoon code-first, but pause at each architectural jump so the room does not lose the plot.
:::
